decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who committed a non-violent financial crime. While embezzlement is a serious breach of fiduciary trust, the defendant has shown remorse and has made efforts to restitute the stolen funds, which are significant mitigating factors. Taking into account relevant sentencing guidelines for white-collar crime of this magnitude, a 6-month period of incarceration is appropriate to reflect the seriousness of the offense while allowing for rehabilitation.
